[2019-10-20 01:02] elyot: a good defensive unit presents a new optimization challenge to both the attacker and the defender

[2019-10-22 22:45] sano712 (sano): Prismata is slightly blurry on fullscreen mode on my macbook, is this a known issue and is there a simple workaround?
[2019-10-22 22:46] elyot: The game is entirely bitmap graphics (no vector graphics except for text) and it has 2 different resolutions optimized for 1024x600 and 1920x1080. If your resolution is different than one of those then it uses cubic spline interpolation to readjust the size of the graphics
[2019-10-22 22:47] elyot: It can sometimes produce a bad result if the resolution is just barely off of 1080p
[2019-10-22 22:47] sano712 (sano): That makes sense, but manually setting it to 1920x1080 does not fix the blur
[2019-10-22 22:47] elyot: send me a screenshot and I can take a look if something is amiss
[2019-10-22 22:47] elyot: also check in options that you didn't force SD graphics
[2019-10-22 22:47] elyot: you can pick HD vs SD
[2019-10-22 22:49] sano712 (sano): Idk how visible it is, but here is a screenshot
[2019-10-22 22:49] sano712 (sano): It's not very visible because the image is reduced :/
[2019-10-22 22:49] elyot: I mean take a screenshot of the game when it's fullscreened and send it to me
[2019-10-22 22:49] elyot: and I will examine the blur and determine the problem
[2019-10-22 22:53] sano712 (sano): https://imgur.com/a/Wjef7Y8
[2019-10-22 22:53] sano712 (sano): Does this work? If you zoom it you can see the blur
[2019-10-22 22:54] elyot: that's a 1024x640 image and it's correct for that size
[2019-10-22 22:54] elyot: I don't think that's the fullscreen resolution of your macbook though
[2019-10-22 22:55] elyot: retina screens are annoying to debug sometimes
[2019-10-22 22:55] sano712 (sano): How do I send the original image without losing quality
[2019-10-22 22:55] elyot: because they try to act like a smaller resolution than they are sometimes
[2019-10-22 22:55] sano712 (sano): The original is 2880x1800
[2019-10-22 22:55] sano712 (sano): But interestingly, if I choose 1920x1080, Prismata gets larger than my screen
[2019-10-22 22:58] elyot: I feel like the mac is trying to do something smart when in fullscreen mode and it's just like, telling prismata it's a tiny screen
[2019-10-22 22:58] elyot: I really know nothing at all about debugging on a mac so I'm kinda at a loss
[2019-10-22 22:58] sano712 (sano): Yeah it's probably more on the mac than on prismata I feel
[2019-10-22 22:59] elyot: we just call some Adobe AIR API that tells us how big the game is sized
[2019-10-22 22:59] elyot: and then load the right graphics based on that
[2019-10-22 22:59] sano712 (sano): It's not really annoying, tbh

[2019-12-18 22:16] elyot: We're happy to have the freeloaders as long as they don't repel new players from the game 😛
[2019-12-18 22:17] elyot: subscription models are pretty dead in this business I'm afraid

[2019-12-18 22:20] elyot: wow has been subscription for a long time but sometime in the early/mid 2000s a lot of companies tried to copy that model and failed, the free-to-play MMOs dominated

[2019-12-18 22:23] Deleted User (Deleted User): were there any on the level of rs?
[2019-12-18 22:23] elyot: like literally every nexon game? idk I don't play those
[2019-12-18 22:24] elyot: A lot of the innovation in f2p business models came from Chinese and Korean MMOs
[2019-12-18 22:25] elyot: WOW was basically the only subscription MMO that achieved large-scale success

[2019-12-18 22:30] Deleted User (Deleted User): I guess they now have wheel of fortune or some other shit
[2019-12-18 22:30] elyot: I would guess that they have a lot of whale-tier players that generate a large amont more revenue than the subscription alone

[2019-12-18 22:32] Deleted User (Deleted User): but then it seemed in '12 they cracked
[2019-12-18 22:32] byron.krane (Keriel): I have seen news/articles about RS players spending numbers of money with more digits than one would expect from a subscription game
[2019-12-18 22:33] jamberine: maybe prismata needs some p2w :LongNoseLiar:
[2019-12-18 22:35] elyot: we should just go full world of tanks where they sell premium ammo for your tank that literally does more damage o_O
[2019-12-18 22:37] elyot: (honestly I don't understand the customers of those kind of games and I think any effort to market to them would be faaaar outside my scope of expertise and would require designing the game from the ground up with that kind of monetization in mind)
[2019-12-18 22:39] Deleted User (Deleted User): i am curious why microtransactions are so popular nowadays hmm. Seems the # of $ is microscopic
[2019-12-18 22:40] awaclus: if you want macroscopic # of $, you need to have macrotransactions :ThinkTank:
[2019-12-18 22:40] elyot: it's mostly because of price discrimination, like if you can get $25 from all customers that are willing to pay $25 then that's fine. But if you can get $10 from people who are willing to pay $10, and $100 from people who are willing to pay $100, then it's much better.
[2019-12-18 22:41] Deleted User (Deleted User): is that basically humble bundle
[2019-12-18 22:41] elyot: no it's more like... why a bunch of games are designed with annoyances that you can pay to remove
[2019-12-18 22:41] byron.krane (Keriel): humble bundle is a valid example, as are steam sales
[2019-12-18 22:41] byron.krane (Keriel): people who wouldn't buy the game at full price still give the dev some money
[2019-12-18 22:42] elyot: the key element is the high end, i.e. having a way that people can give you unlimited (or a large finite amount) of money
[2019-12-18 22:42] elyot: which is why a full set of Hearthstone golden cards costs like... tens of thousands
[2019-12-18 22:42] Deleted User (Deleted User): so basically prismatic skins :ThinkTank:
[2019-12-18 22:43] Deleted User (Deleted User): (i'm not sure this would be commericially the best but i'm throwing it out there for selfish reasons) you should work on the competitive/tournament scene, I know i'd love to come back in if I can make a career with pris winnings
[2019-12-18 22:43] elyot: the low end is mostly important because it provides a way of getting more players, and even players that pay $0 have value, both because they fill the queues and build the community, and because they can invite others to play
[2019-12-18 22:44] Deleted User (Deleted User): and mr coffeeyay can stake me
[2019-12-18 22:44] byron.krane (Keriel): also re earlier discussion of not understanding those kinds of games: from what I can tell a lot of people just don't have a strong sense of fairness in games, or at least that's the only way I can explain something like Dead by Daylight which (as I understand it, I don't play it) has a ranked mode with in-game consumables that give you advantages in those matches but has this even though you can't pay RL money for them. I don't understand it either but this was inconsistent with my prior theories which is why I am mentioning it
[2019-12-18 22:44] Deleted User (Deleted User): have u got any ideas why going f2p hasn't generated a big bump in player #
[2019-12-18 22:45] elyot: "esports to grow a game's audience" has largely proven to be an incorrect business idea
[2019-12-18 22:45] elyot: which is why blizzard cut a lot of their esports budget
[2019-12-18 22:45] elyot: it's more of a value-add for existing players rather than something that monetizes them or generates new players
[2019-12-18 22:46] Deleted User (Deleted User): byron wdym not understanding those kinds of games? a lot of chat had scrolled past and I can't remember what was said
[2019-12-18 22:46] Deleted User (Deleted User): that's fascinating. So why do so many games have big tournament scenes
[2019-12-18 22:46] Deleted User (Deleted User): I guess even more games don't so eh
[2019-12-18 22:47] Deleted User (Deleted User): like if there was pris and a pris that was slightly shittier or a pris but slightly better i would still go for the one which paid me
[2019-12-18 22:47] byron.krane (Keriel): (Elyot said he did not understand the players of games like WoT)
[2019-12-18 22:47] elyot: I think the answer to Byron's objection is like... the golf analogy. You can pay for better golf clubs and balls and people actually like doing this once golf is their hobby
